title: Potter Creek & Samwel Caves — 3-D archive of the survey record
principle: Every item is a faithful vector trace of a real published drawing, fully cited. No AI imagery; no invented geometry.
pipeline: trace_drawing.sh (pdftoppm -> crop -> threshold -> potrace -> SVG); svg_to_x3d.py / extrude_contours.py for to-scale X3D.

entries.subject: Potter Creek Cave — topographic plan of the chamber floor
entries.citation: Sinclair, W.J. (1904) The Exploration of the Potter Creek Cave, Plate 14 ("Topographic map of the floor of the main chamber… contour interval 6 inches"). Univ. Calif. Publ. Amer. Arch. Ethn. 2(1):1-27.

entries.subject: Potter Creek Cave — longitudinal section of the buried gallery
entries.citation: Sinclair, W.J. (1904), Plate 11. Univ. Calif. Publ. Amer. Arch. Ethn. 2(1).

entries.subject: Potter Creek Cave — deposit cross-sections 1–6
entries.citation: Sinclair, W.J. (1904), Plates 12–13. Univ. Calif. Publ. Amer. Arch. Ethn. 2(1).

entries.subject: Samwel Cave — cross-sectional view
entries.citation: Feranec, R.S., Hadly, E.A., Blois, J.L., Barnosky, A.D. & Paytan, A. (2007) Radiocarbon Dates from the Pleistocene Fossil Deposits of Samwel Cave, Figure 2. Radiocarbon 49(1):117-121.

entries.subject: Potter Creek Cave — 3-D floor topography (derived)
entries.citation: Derived from Sinclair, W.J. (1904), Plate 14 — contours lifted by nesting depth × the surveyed 6-inch interval and triangulated.

entries.subject: Potter Creek Cave — bone-horizon section + interpretive hero (derived)
entries.citation: Sinclair 1904 (NW-fan column S–H + the faunal 'unit', p.19); Payen & Taylor 1976 (Euceratherium radius+ulna at 170 cm, 8250±330 BP, UCR-381; polished 'bone tools' human origin refuted, p.56).
entries.source_url: see A1; Payen & Taylor 1976, J. Calif. Anthropology 3(1):51-58 (eScholarship qt8zs315nk)
entries.note: Fauna pinned ONLY where documented; mixed assemblage shown as one 'throughout, not depth-sorted' marker; sloth hero flagged interpretive/unprovenanced.

entries.subject: Samwel Cave — per-specimen dated levels, 'depth != age' (derived)
entries.citation: Feranec, Hadly, Blois, Barnosky & Paytan (2007) Radiocarbon 49(1):117-121, Table 2 (four Chamber-Two AMS dates at real square+inch levels; deposit not chronologically stratified).

entries.subject: Euceratherium collinum (shrub-ox) — left superior dental series
entries.citation: Sinclair, W.J. & Furlong, E.L. (1904) Euceratherium, a new ungulate from the Quaternary caves of California, Text Fig. 1 ("Left superior dental series, x1/3"). Univ. Calif. Bull. Dept. Geol. 3(20):411-418. Type locality: Potter Creek Cave (type cranium UCMP 8751). [Pls. 50-51 are halftone photographs, not line drawings.]

entries.subject: Nothrotheriops shastensis (Shasta ground sloth) — lateral skeleton
entries.citation: Stock, C. (1925) Cenozoic Gravigrade Edentates of Western North America, Fig. 4 (skeleton in lateral view). Carnegie Institution of Washington Publication 331, p. 33.
entries.note: The hero — a full articulated skeletal line drawing.

entries.subject: Canis dirus (dire wolf) — lateral skull
entries.citation: Merriam, J.C. (1912) The Fauna of Rancho La Brea, Part II: Canidae, text-fig. 1 (skull no. 10834, lateral, x1/2). Memoirs of the University of California 1(2), p. 224.

entries.subject: Located, not yet traced
entries.mammuthus: Osborn, H.F. (1942) Proboscidea vol. 2, Figs. 996-998, p. 1130 (archive.org proboscideamonog02osbo, leaf 364).
entries.camelops: from the same fossil hunt; exact figure pending.
entries.arctodus_simus: No line drawing exists; Merriam & Stock (1925), Carnegie Publ. 347, figures the skull/skeleton only as photographs. Skipped under the no-AI / real-drawing rule.
